yesterdayStoryline.headline: Mexico open with win as record three reds mar curtain-raiser
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: El Tri beat South Africa 2-0 at the Azteca; South Korea fought back to edge Czechia and sit level atop Group A.

todayStoryline.headline: Canada christen home World Cup; USA begin against Paraguay
todayStoryline.subtitle: Canada play the first men's World Cup match on home soil against Bosnia; the USMNT open Group D in Los Angeles.

yesterday.note: 🟥 Sithole 49', Zwane 84' (RSA) · Montes 90+2' (MEX) · WC opener red-card record

lines: Mexico dominated possession at 60% and pressed from a fluid 4-1-4-1 shape.
It is Mexico's first win in eight World Cup openers, amid a record three reds.
South Korea built through possession, completing 464 passes to Czechia's 242.
The comeback lifts Korea level with Mexico atop Group A on three points.

facts.home: This is the first men's World Cup match ever played on Canadian soil.
facts.away: Bosnia reached the finals by beating four-time champions Italy in a play-off shootout.

facts.home: The USA's first World Cup match was a 3-0 win over Paraguay in 1930.
facts.away: Paraguay return to the World Cup for the first time since 2010.

news.headline: Trump calls off fresh Iran strikes, says ceasefire deal could come within days
news.body: President Trump said he had cancelled planned military strikes on Iran and that Washington was close to a settlement to wind down the war. He said a memorandum of understanding extending the April ceasefire could be signed within days, allowing more time for nuclear talks.

20260612.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

todayStoryline.headline: World Cup 2026 kicks off in Mexico City; Shakira opens at Estadio Azteca
todayStoryline.subtitle: Mexico face South Africa — a reverse of the 2010 opener that ended 1-1. South Korea v Czechia closes day one in the tournament's first 3am UK kickoff.

facts.home: Mexico played the first match in World Cup history — a 4-1 loss to France on 13 July 1930.
facts.away: South Africa's first match as a unified nation came in 1992 after a 16-year FIFA ban.

facts.home: Their 2002 semi-final run remains Asia's deepest finish at any World Cup.
facts.away: Predecessor Czechoslovakia reached two World Cup finals (1934, 1962) and lost both.

news.headline: Trump warns Iran 'will pay the price' as US strikes follow Apache downing
news.body: The US military completed strikes against Iran in response to a downed Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, with Trump saying Tehran has stalled ceasefire talks. Israeli operations in southern Lebanon overnight killed at least 13 people.

20260611.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: US dominates Paraguay in opener
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Hosts secure 4-1 victory with Balogun's brace

todayStoryline.headline: Brazil faces Morocco challenge
todayStoryline.subtitle: Seleção seeks strong start against resilient Atlas Lions

lines: Canada secured their first World Cup point with a late equalizer.
Bosnia and Herzegovina's early lead was nullified by Larin's impact.
USA's aggressive play led to a commanding 4-1 victory.
Balogun's double highlighted the hosts' attacking prowess.

facts.home: Qatar's first World Cup match against a European team.
facts.away: Switzerland's first World Cup match in the Middle East.

facts.home: Brazil has never lost to an African team in World Cup history.
facts.away: Morocco's first World Cup match against Brazil.

facts.home: Haiti's first World Cup appearance since 1974.
facts.away: Scotland's first World Cup match outside Europe.

facts.home: Australia's first World Cup match against a European team since 2014.
facts.away: Türkiye's first World Cup match since 2002.

news.headline: Global leaders convene for climate summit
news.body: World leaders gather to discuss urgent climate action. The summit aims to address rising global temperatures and emissions.

20260613.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: Late levellers and early shocks reshape groups
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Qatar and Morocco held favourites, Scotland edged Haiti, and Australia punished Türkiye in Vancouver.

todayStoryline.headline: Curaçao debut frames Group E start
todayStoryline.subtitle: Germany open against the smallest World Cup qualifier before Group F tests depth and balance.

lines: Switzerland created heavily but failed to settle the match after Embolo's penalty.
Qatar's late point left Group B level after the opening round.
Morocco pressed through Brazil's midfield before Vinícius produced the equaliser against the run of play.
Brazil avoided defeat, but Ancelotti's side opened with clear structural concerns.
Scotland struck from a direct move and then survived Haiti's late pressure.
The win put Scotland above Brazil and Morocco in Group C.
Australia defended deep, rode Patrick Beach's saves, and punished Türkiye turnovers.
The Socceroos joined the United States on three points in Group D.

facts.home: Joachim Löw was filmed picking his nose on live World Cup TV more than once in 2018.
facts.away: Curaçao is the smallest nation ever to qualify — population around 150,000, less than a London borough.

facts.home: Memphis Depay once kept a pet lion called Trinity until animal-welfare groups convinced him otherwise.
facts.away: Japanese fans famously tidy stadiums after their team's games — the squad cleans the dressing room too.

facts.home: Yaya Touré's agent once publicly complained Manchester City gave his star a 'disrespectfully small' birthday cake.
facts.away: Ecuador play home qualifiers at 2,850m in Quito, leaving visiting teams gasping by halftime.

facts.home: Zlatan Ibrahimović has held a taekwondo black belt since he was 17.
facts.away: Tunisia were the first African nation to win a World Cup match, beating Mexico 3-1 in 1978.

news.headline: UK says forces intercepted Russian shadow-fleet tanker
news.body: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he directed armed forces to intercept a shadow-fleet oil tanker attempting to pass through the English Channel. He framed the operation as a move against networks helping fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

20260614.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: Germany score seven, Japan salvage late point
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Group E opened with German authority; Group F already looks more congested.

todayStoryline.headline: Spain start Group H, Belgium open Group G
todayStoryline.subtitle: Four fixtures stretch from Atlanta to Los Angeles, with Uruguay favoured in Miami.

yesterday.scorers: Felix Nmecha 6' (GER), Nico Schlotterbeck 38' (GER), Kai Havertz 45+5' pen, 88' (GER), Jamal Musiala 47' (GER), Nathaniel Brown 68' (GER), Denis Undav 78' (GER) · Livano Comenencia 21' (CUW)

lines: Germany’s front line found balance after Curaçao briefly levelled through a historic first World Cup goal.
The margin puts immediate pressure on Ivory Coast and Ecuador in Group E.

yesterday.scorers: Virgil van Dijk 51' (NED), Crysencio Summerville 64' (NED) · Keito Nakamura 57' (JPN), Daichi Kamada 89' (JPN)

lines: The match stayed flat before four second-half goals turned it into a late exchange.
Japan’s point keeps Group F open after the Netherlands failed to manage their lead.
Ecuador struck the woodwork twice early before Ivory Coast improved after attacking changes.
Amad’s late winner gives Ivory Coast a useful Group E platform before Germany.

yesterday.scorers: Yasin Ayari 7', 90+6' (SWE), Alexander Isak 30' (SWE), Viktor Gyökeres 59' (SWE), Mattias Svanberg 84' (SWE) · Omar Rekik 43' (TUN)

lines: Sweden’s forwards combined cleanly and Ayari’s long-range finishing stretched Tunisia throughout.
The five-goal win sends Sweden top of Group F after one round.

facts.home: Marc Cucurella dyed his famous curls red after promising it for Spain’s Euro win.
facts.away: Cape Verde boss Bubista’s nickname simply comes from Boa Vista, his home island.

facts.home: Kevin De Bruyne once compared baking a cake to crafting the perfect pass.
facts.away: Salah once joked his PlayStation version is stronger than the real Mohamed Salah.

facts.home: Hervé Renard’s lucky white shirt became almost as famous as Saudi Arabia’s upset.
facts.away: Luis Suárez’s statue in Salto once ‘left’ a note for Messi’s wedding.

facts.home: Iran’s Team Melli nickname is wonderfully literal: it simply means ‘the national team’.
facts.away: New Zealand’s 1982 qualifiers involved 15 matches and about 55,000 travel miles.

news.headline: Britain announces sweeping social media ban for under-16s
news.body: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Britain would ban under-16s from social media sites and add restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms. The government framed the move as a child-safety measure, while the policy places Britain among the countries tightening rules on young people’s online access.

20260615.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: Four draws stall Groups G and H
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Spain and Cape Verde stayed scoreless, while three other fixtures were shared.

todayStoryline.headline: Groups I and J open with contrasts
todayStoryline.subtitle: France revisit Senegal history before Argentina begin their title defence in Kansas City.

facts.home: Griezmann adored Uruguay so much he drank mate and backed Peñarol from afar.
facts.away: Mané once asked why anyone needs ten Ferraris, twenty watches or two planes.

facts.home: Ahmed Radhi’s 1986 strike remains Iraq’s only World Cup finals goal.
facts.away: Haaland once rapped with Flow Kingz before becoming Norway’s striking Viking.

facts.home: Messi’s first Barcelona promise was famously scribbled on a restaurant napkin.
facts.away: Rabah Madjer’s audacious backheel in 1987 gave its name to a skill.

facts.home: Austria’s 7-5 win over Switzerland remains the World Cup’s wildest scoreline.
facts.away: Musa Al-Taamari is nicknamed the Jordanian Messi, though he dislikes the label.

news.headline: European leaders press Trump on Ukraine strategy at G7
news.body: European leaders planned to urge U.S. President Donald Trump at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains to rethink his approach to Ukraine. They also warned that any interim Iran deal should not entrench Tehran’s nuclear and missile programmes.

20260616.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: Messi reaches Klose as favourites settle
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Argentina’s captain matched the World Cup scoring record, while Mbappé and Haaland opened with doubles.

todayStoryline.headline: Portugal start, England revisit Croatia
todayStoryline.subtitle: Four openers stretch from Houston to Mexico City, with first meetings framing Group K.

yesterday.scorers: Kylian Mbappé 66', 90+6' (FRA) · Bradley Barcola 82' (FRA) · Ibrahim Mbaye 90+5' (SEN)

lines: Senegal held France for an hour before Mbappé and Barcola punished tiring legs.
France join Norway on three points in Group I after the opening round.

yesterday.scorers: Aymen Hussein 39' (IRQ) · Erling Haaland 29', 43' (NOR) · Leo Østigård 76' (NOR) · Aymen Hussein 90+6' OG (NOR)

lines: Iraq competed well before errors and Haaland’s movement stretched the match away.
Norway sit level with France at the top of Group I.
Messi’s hat-trick turned a controlled Argentina performance into a record-equalling night.
Argentina lead Group J on goal difference after the first fixtures.

yesterday.scorers: Romano Schmid 20' (AUT) · Yazan Al-Arab 75' OG (AUT) · Marko Arnautović 101' pen (AUT) · Ali Olwan 49' (JOR)

lines: Jordan threatened after half-time, but Austria’s set-pieces and late penalty decided it.
Austria trail Argentina on goal difference in Group J.

facts.home: Pepe’s full name honours scientists Johannes Kepler and Charles Louis Alphonse Laveran.
facts.away: Mwepu Ilunga once charged from Zaire’s wall and booted Brazil’s free-kick away.

facts.home: Pickford has kept penalty tips taped to his water bottle for club and country.
facts.away: Croatia’s red-and-white checks also decorate the national coat of arms.

facts.home: Kevin-Prince Boateng released a rap single under the name PRIN$$ Boateng.
facts.away: Panama’s 2018 qualification was celebrated with a presidentially declared national holiday.

facts.home: Abbosbek Fayzullaev became Uzbekistan’s second AFC Youth Player of the Year.
facts.away: René Higuita’s scorpion kick at Wembley remains Colombia’s ultimate goalkeeper party trick.

news.headline: Qinghai quake kills one, injures four
news.body: A magnitude 6.3 earthquake struck China’s northwestern Qinghai province on Tuesday, killing at least one person and injuring four. Rescue teams searched affected areas after the shallow quake, with thousands of residents potentially exposed to shaking.

20260617.footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs

yesterdayStoryline.headline: Colombia capitalise as Portugal stumble
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Group K tilted late, while England and Ghana both opened Group L with wins.

todayStoryline.headline: Hosts Canada and Mexico return to centre stage
todayStoryline.subtitle: Groups A and B resume with home crowds, awkward matchups and little separation.

lines: Portugal led early but control became sterile as DR Congo countered and attacked set pieces.
The draw left Group K open before Colombia took the lead later.

yesterday.scorers: Harry Kane 10' pen, 42' (ENG) · Martin Baturina 36' (CRO) · Petar Musa 45+1' (CRO) · Jude Bellingham 47' (ENG) · Marcus Rashford 85' (ENG)

lines: England twice lost first-half leads, then accelerated after Tuchel urged a braver restart.
Kane levelled Lineker’s World Cup mark as England opened Group L with control.
Panama dragged Ghana into a tight, physical contest until stoppage time broke it.
Yirenkyi’s late goal gives Ghana early leverage in Group L.

yesterday.scorers: Daniel Muñoz 40' (COL) · Abbosbek Fayzullaev 60' (UZB) · Luis Díaz 65' (COL) · Jaminton Campaz 90+9' (COL)

lines: Uzbekistan responded well after halftime, but Díaz’s quality restored Colombia’s edge quickly.
Colombia finished the night top of Group K after Portugal’s earlier draw.

facts.home: Petr Čech swapped football gloves for ice-hockey pads with Guildford Phoenix after retiring.
facts.away: Tshabalala’s 2010 opener came with a synchronised dance now baked into World Cup folklore.

facts.home: Shaqiri’s old ‘Alpine Messi’ nickname somehow shared shelf space with ‘magic dwarf’.
facts.away: Bosnia boss Sergej Barbarez went from Bundesliga goals to professional poker tables.

facts.home: Alphonso Davies became football’s TikTok king with one-man Brooklyn Nine-Nine sketches.
facts.away: Qatar’s 2022 squad once spent a very unusual six months in camp together.

facts.home: Memo Ochoa once had to outlive a bizarre internet hoax about six fingers.
facts.away: Son Heung-min completed military training and reportedly won the best-trainee award.

news.headline: G7 seeks critical-minerals shield against China reliance
news.body: G7 leaders worked on a statement aimed at reducing Western dependence on China for critical minerals. The plan under discussion included measures to protect investment and guard against market pressure.

yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Switzerland dominate Bosnia as Mexico secure early knockout spot in Group A

facts.home: The 1994 World Cup in the USA had the highest average attendance, about 69,000 per match.
facts.away: Tim Cahill famously shadow‑boxed the corner flag as his trademark goal celebration.

facts.home: Scotland fans famously belt out 'Flower of Scotland' at every big away match.
facts.away: Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach a World Cup semi‑final in 2022.

facts.home: Pelé was named after Thomas Edison; his parents removed the 'i', recording him as Edson.
facts.away: Emmanuel 'Manno' Sanon famously ended Dino Zoff's long World Cup clean‑sheet run in 1974.

facts.home: Hakan Şükür scored the fastest World Cup goal, after about 11 seconds, in 2002.
facts.away: José Luis Chilavert, Paraguay's keeper, was famous for scoring free‑kicks and penalties.

news.headline: U.S. Senate advances emergency foreign‑aid package amid budget talks
news.body: Senators moved forward with a bipartisan bill to approve emergency foreign assistance as budget negotiations continued. The measure would provide short‑term funding while negotiators hammer out a wider fiscal agreement.
